name: page-constructor
description: For landing/marketing pages only. Block-based page builder providing 40+ content blocks (headers, media, cards, forms, tables, maps) for composing marketing and documentation pages from YAML/JSON configs.
when_to_use: For landing/marketing pages only
avoid: Not for application UI — use uikit. Not for blog pages — use blog-constructor. Not for dynamic server-rendered app pages.

name: Anchor
description: Renders an invisible anchor div with an id, used for in-page navigation and scroll-to-section links.
when_to_use: When you need a named anchor point for in-page links
When a block needs a scrollable target
avoid: Do not use as a visible UI element; it renders no visible content

props.name: id
props.description: The HTML id for the anchor element.

examples: <Anchor id="features-section" />

name: AnimateBlock
description: Wraps content in a scroll-triggered animation container using react-waypoint. Adds CSS animation classes when the block enters the viewport.
when_to_use: When a section should animate in as the user scrolls to it
When you want entrance animations for page blocks
avoid: Avoid when animations should always play on mount rather than on scroll
Avoid when SSR is critical and animation triggers are not needed

props.name: animate
props.description: Whether to enable animation; defaults to the AnimateContext value.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class name for the wrapper.

props.name: offset
props.description: Bottom offset in pixels before triggering animation (default 100).

props.name: onScroll
props.description: Callback fired when the block enters the viewport.

examples: Content that fades in on scroll

name: Author
description: Displays an author's avatar, name, and optional description. Supports both column and row layout types.
when_to_use: When showing post or article authorship
When displaying a person's avatar and name in a card
avoid: Avoid for generic user avatars without the authorship context; prefer uikit Avatar instead

props.name: author
props.description: Author data object with firstName, secondName, description, and avatar.

props.name: authorContainerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the avatar container.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the root element.

examples:
<Author
  author={{
    firstName: 'Lorem',
    secondName: 'Ipsum',
    description: 'Lorem Ipsum',
    avatar: '/path/to/avatar.png'
  }}
/>

<Author
  author={{
    firstName: 'Lorem',
    secondName: 'Ipsum',
    description: 'Lorem Ipsum',
    avatar: '/path/to/avatar.png'
  }}
  type="line"
/>

name: BackgroundImage
description: Renders a section with a background image and an optional content overlay. Supports desktop/mobile responsive sources.
when_to_use: When a section needs a full-bleed background image with content layered on top
avoid: Avoid for simple inline images; use Image instead

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the outer container.

props.name: imageClassName
props.description: CSS class for the image element.

examples:
<BackgroundImage
  src="/path/to/header-bg.png"
  alt="Background"
  disableCompress={true}
  style={{ height: '500px' }}
/>

name: BackgroundMedia
description: Renders a full-width background media container supporting images, video, and parallax effects. Wraps Media in an AnimateBlock.
when_to_use: When a page block needs a rich background with image or video and optional parallax
When combining background color with media
avoid: Avoid for inline content images; use Media or Image instead

props.name: fullWidthMedia
props.description: Extend media to full container width.

props.name: mediaClassName
props.description: CSS class for the media element.

examples:
<BackgroundMedia
  image={{
    desktop: '/path/to/header-bg.png',
    mobile: '/path/to/mobile-bg.png',
    alt: 'Picture',
    disableCompress: true
  }}
/>

<BackgroundMedia
  video={{
    src: ['/path/to/video.mp4', '/path/to/video.webm'],
    loop: { start: 0 }
  }}
  image="/path/to/fallback.png"
/>

name: BalancedMasonry
description: A masonry layout that balances column heights by distributing children into columns based on their measured heights and responsive breakpoints.
when_to_use: When displaying variable-height cards in a multi-column layout that should look visually balanced
avoid: Avoid when all items have equal heights; use a regular CSS grid instead
Avoid in SSR-only contexts where ResizeObserver is not available

props.name: breakpointCols
props.description: Map of max-width breakpoints to column counts.

props.name: columnClassName
props.description: CSS class applied to each column.

examples:
<BalancedMasonry
  className="masonry-container"
  columnClassName="masonry-column"
  breakpointCols={{
    576: 1,
    768: 2,
    992: 2,
    1200: 3,
    1400: 3
  }}
>
  <CardBase><CardBase.Content>Card 1</CardBase.Content></CardBase>
  <CardBase><CardBase.Content>Card 2</CardBase.Content></CardBase>
  <CardBase><CardBase.Content>Card 3</CardBase.Content></CardBase>
</BalancedMasonry>

name: BlockBase
description: Base wrapper for all page blocks that applies standard vertical indentation, optional anchor, and visibility control.
when_to_use: When building a custom page block that needs standard spacing and optional anchor support
avoid: Avoid using directly for non-block content; it is intended as a layout primitive for page sections
examples: Block content with standard spacing
Anchored block with id for in-page navigation

name: BrandFooter
description: Renders a branded footer link to gravity-ui.com with the Gravity UI brand icon and name, theme-aware (light/dark).
when_to_use: When the page is built with PageConstructor and isBranded is true
avoid: Avoid adding manually unless intentional brand attribution is desired
examples: <BrandFooter />
<PageConstructor content={pageContent} isBranded={true} />

name: Button
description: Page-constructor button component that wraps uikit Button with extended theme support including github, app-store, google-play, and scale variants. Supports optional icon/image.
when_to_use: When rendering a CTA button from page content data
When you need app-store or github-branded buttons
avoid: Avoid for interactive UI controls outside page content; use @gravity-ui/uikit Button directly instead
examples: <Button text="Get started" theme="action" url="https://example.com" size="l" />
<Button text="Download on App Store" theme="app-store" url="https://apps.apple.com/..." />
<Button text="View on GitHub" theme="github" url="https://github.com/gravity-ui" />

name: ButtonTabs
description: Renders a horizontal row of tab buttons with accessible role=tablist. Manages active tab state and fires onSelectTab callbacks.
when_to_use: When building a tabbed content switcher using button-style tabs
avoid: Avoid for complex tab panels with mixed content; use uikit Tabs instead

props.name: items
props.description: Tab items array with id and title.

props.name: getTabContentElementId
props.description: Returns aria-controls value for a tab.

props.name: getTabElementId
props.description: Returns the tab button element id.

props.name: onSelectTab
props.description: Callback when a tab is selected.

examples:
<ButtonTabs
  items={[
    { id: 'tab1', title: 'Overview' },
    { id: 'tab2', title: 'Features' },
    { id: 'tab3', title: 'Pricing' }
  ]}
  activeTab="tab1"
  onSelectTab={(tabId) => setActiveTab(tabId)}
/>

name: Buttons
description: Renders a list of Button components from an array of ButtonProps, with size derived from content size ('s' | 'l').
when_to_use: When rendering multiple CTA buttons from page block data
avoid: Avoid for a single button; use Button directly

props.name: titleId
props.description: Id of a title element for aria-describedby.

examples:
<Buttons
  buttons={[
    { text: 'Get started', theme: 'action', url: 'https://example.com' },
    { text: 'Learn more', theme: 'outlined', url: '#' }
  ]}
  size="l"
/>

name: CardBase
description: Composite card layout component with Header, Content, and Footer sub-components. Supports border styles and an optional URL that wraps the card in a link.
when_to_use: When building structured cards with distinct header image, content area, and footer
When a card should be entirely clickable as a link
avoid: Avoid for simple content without header/footer structure; use a plain div instead

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the root element.

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: CSS class for the content area.

props.name: extraProps
props.description: Extra props for the card element.

props.name: url
props.description: If provided, wraps card in a link.

examples:
Card Title

Card description text

<CardBase url="https://example.com" urlTitle="Read more" border="line">
  <CardBase.Content>Clickable card content</CardBase.Content>
</CardBase>

name: ContentList
description: Renders a list of items each with an optional icon, title heading, and YFM-formatted text. Heading level adapts to content size.
when_to_use: When displaying feature lists or bullet-point sections with optional icons and titles
avoid: Avoid for simple unordered lists without icons or titles; use plain HTML ul instead

props.name: list
props.description: Array of list items with icon, title, and text.

examples:
<ContentList
  size="l"
  list={[
    {
      icon: { light: '/icons/icon1_light.svg', dark: '/icons/icon1_dark.svg' },
      title: 'Feature One',
      text: 'Description of feature one with **bold** and [link](https://example.com).'
    },
    {
      icon: { light: '/icons/icon2_light.svg', dark: '/icons/icon2_dark.svg' },
      title: 'Feature Two',
      text: 'Description of feature two.'
    }
  ]}
/>

name: Control
description: A small icon-only button with configurable theme and size, used as a toolbar or control action button.
when_to_use: When you need a small icon-only action button with page-constructor theming
avoid: Avoid for labeled buttons; use Button instead
examples:
<Control
  icon={emailIconSvgString}
  theme="primary"
  size="s"
  iconSize={16}
  onClick={() => console.log('control clicked')}
/>

name: ErrorWrapper
description: Conditionally renders an error state with a message and retry button, or its children when no error is present.
when_to_use: When wrapping async content that may fail and needs a user-visible error with a retry action
avoid: Avoid as a general React error boundary; it is not a class-based ErrorBoundary and does not catch render errors

props.name: isError
props.description: When true, shows the error UI instead of children.

examples:
refetch()} >
Content that loaded successfully

<ErrorWrapper
  text="Temporary error — click retry to fix"
  isError={hasError}
  buttonText="Fix"
  handler={() => window.location.reload()}
>
  Hidden content when error resolves
</ErrorWrapper>

name: FileLink
description: Renders a downloadable file link with a color-coded extension label (PDF, DOC, XLS, PPT, FIG, ZIP) and link text.
when_to_use: When displaying links to downloadable files with visual file-type indicators
avoid: Avoid for regular navigation links; use Link instead

props.name: href
props.description: URL of the file to download.

examples: <FileLink href="document.pdf" text="Download PDF" />
<FileLink href="report.xls" text="Download Report" type="horizontal" theme="default" />

name: Foldable
description: An animated collapsible container that smoothly expands and collapses its children using measured content height.
when_to_use: When content should be toggled open/closed with a smooth height animation
avoid: Avoid when CSS-only transitions are sufficient; this component uses JS height measurement
examples: Collapsible content that animates height

name: FullWidthBackground
description: A class component that dynamically stretches its background to the full browser width, compensating for parent container padding, with support for a rounded theme.
when_to_use: When a section's background must extend to the browser edges while content stays within a grid
avoid: Avoid in SSR contexts without a client-side resize handler; the width is calculated imperatively in componentDidMount
examples: Full-width section content
Rounded full-width section

name: FullscreenImage
description: An image that displays a fullscreen expand button and opens a modal lightbox with the full-size image or an image slider.
when_to_use: When images should be viewable in a full-screen overlay
When displaying an image gallery in a lightbox slider
avoid: Avoid for images where fullscreen is never needed; use Image for simpler cases

props.name: extraProps
props.description: Extra props for the wrapper div.

props.name: imageClassName
props.description: CSS class for the inline image.

props.name: modalImageClass
props.description: CSS class for the modal image.

examples:
<FullscreenImage
  src="/path/to/image.png"
  alt="Description"
  imageStyle={{ maxWidth: '500px' }}
/>

name: FullscreenMedia
description: A render-prop wrapper that adds a fullscreen expand button to any media content, opening it in a modal. On mobile it renders children directly without modal support.
when_to_use: When any media component (video, image) should support fullscreen viewing on desktop
avoid: Avoid on mobile-only contexts; the component bypasses the modal on mobile

props.name: children
props.description: Render prop receiving media class names and fullscreen state.

props.name: showFullscreenIcon
props.description: Whether to show the expand icon button (default true).

examples:
<FullscreenMedia>
  {(fullscreenProps = {}) => (
    <Media
      youtube="https://youtu.be/0Qd3T6skprA"
      previewImg="/path/to/preview.png"
      {...fullscreenProps}
    />
  )}
</FullscreenMedia>

name: HTML
description: Renders HTML content via dangerouslySetInnerHTML with configurable tag name, and supports optional children positioned before or after the HTML content.
when_to_use: When rendering sanitized HTML strings from a CMS or content pipeline
avoid: Avoid for untrusted HTML; ensure content is sanitized before passing
Avoid when React children alone suffice

props.name: block
props.description: Use a block tag wrapper (default false).

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the outer wrapper.

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: CSS class for the HTML content element.

props.name: contentPosition
props.description: Position of the HTML content relative to children (default 'start').

props.name: onlyContent
props.description: Render only the HTML content without the outer wrapper (default false).

examples: <HTML content={sanitizedHtml} block={true} className="rich-text" />

name: HeaderBreadcrumbs
description: Renders an accessible breadcrumb navigation (<nav> with <ol>) with analytics event support.
when_to_use: When showing page hierarchy navigation at the top of a content page
avoid: Avoid for tab or step navigation; use ButtonTabs or a stepper instead
examples:
<HeaderBreadcrumbs
  theme="light"
  items={[
    { url: '#', text: 'Lorem ipsum' },
    { url: '#', text: 'Dolor sit amet' },
    { url: '#', text: 'Consectetur adipiscing elit' }
  ]}
/>

name: IconWrapper
description: Wraps content with a positioned icon (image URL or Gravity UI icon), placing the icon at a configurable position relative to the content.
when_to_use: When content needs an icon placed at a specific position (top, left, right) alongside it
avoid: Avoid when no icon is needed; it renders children as a Fragment

props.name: icon
props.description: Image-based icon data with value and position.

examples: Content with icon at top
Content with Gravity UI icon

name: Image
description: A responsive image component using <picture> with device-specific sources for mobile/tablet/desktop, optional WebP conversion, and hide-by-device support.
when_to_use: When rendering page content images with responsive device-specific sources
When WebP optimization should be applied automatically
avoid: Avoid for simple static images with a single source; use a plain instead

props.name: desktop
props.description: Desktop image URL (also used as fallback src).

props.name: hide
props.type: boolean | { mobile?: boolean; tablet?: boolean; desktop?: boolean }

examples:
<Image
  src="/path/to/image.png"
  alt="Description"
  disableCompress={true}
  fetchPriority="high"
  style={{ height: '200px' }}
/>

name: ImageBase
description: Low-level image rendering primitive that delegates to a custom Image component from ImageContext, or falls back to a plain with fetchPriority support.
when_to_use: When you need to use the project-level image override from ImageContext
As the inner image element inside Image or custom image components
avoid: Avoid using directly when Image or BackgroundImage meets the needs
examples: <ImageBase src="/path/to/image.png" alt="Image description" fetchPriority="high" />

name: InnerForm
description: Renders either a YandexForm or HubspotForm based on the formData type, reading configuration from FormsContext.
when_to_use: When embedding a form (Yandex or Hubspot) within a page block using FormBlockData
avoid: Avoid using directly outside of page-constructor form block context; configure via FormsContext

props.name: onContentLoad
props.description: Callback when the form iframe has loaded.

examples:
<InnerForm
  formData={{ id: '61a4e639d4d24e0dbba36f5c', customFormSection: 'cloud' }}
  onContentLoad={() => setFormLoaded(true)}
/>

name: Link
description: Polymorphic link component that renders as a back-link, file-link, or normal anchor based on the theme prop, with analytics and TLD support.
when_to_use: When rendering links from page content data with different visual styles (normal, back, file-link)
avoid: Avoid for router-aware navigation; use RouterLink or a framework link component instead

props.name: arrow
props.description: Show a chevron arrow after the text.

examples: <Link text="Default link" url="#" />
<Link text="Normal link with an arrow" url="#" arrow={true} theme="normal" textSize="m" />
<Link url="#" theme="back">Back to list</Link>

name: Links
description: Renders a collection of Link components from an array of LinkProps with size derived from content size.
when_to_use: When rendering multiple links from page block content data
avoid: Avoid for a single link; use Link directly

props.name: titleId
props.description: Id of a title for aria-describedby.

examples:
<Links
  links={[
    { url: '#', text: 'Link one', theme: 'normal', arrow: true },
    { url: '#', text: 'Link two', theme: 'normal', arrow: true }
  ]}
  size="l"
/>

name: Map
description: Renders a map using either Yandex Maps or Google Maps based on the MapsContext configuration.
when_to_use: When displaying an interactive map in a page block, with map type determined by project config
avoid: Avoid without setting up MapsContext with the correct map type and API keys

props.name: ...MapProps
props.description: Props forwarded to the active map implementation.

examples:
<MapProvider scriptSrc={ymapsScriptSrc} apiKey={apiKey} type={MapType.Yandex}>
  <Map
    id="common-places"
    markers={[
      { address: 'Moscow Arbat', label: { preset: 'islands#circleIcon' } },
      { address: 'Moscow, Red square', label: { preset: 'islands#blackStretchyIcon', iconContent: 'Red square' } }
    ]}
  />
</MapProvider>

<MapProvider scriptSrc={gmapsScriptSrc} type={MapType.Google} apiKey={googleApiKey}>
  <Map address="Anthony Fokkerweg 1, 1059 CM Amsterdam" />
</MapProvider>

name: Media
description: Universal media container that renders image, video, YouTube iframe, DataLens chart, or custom iframe based on the provided props.
when_to_use: When rendering content that can be any media type (image, video, YouTube, iframe) based on data
avoid: Avoid when only a specific media type is needed; use Image or VideoBlock directly for simpler cases

props.name: youtubeClassName
props.description: CSS class for the YouTube iframe.

examples:
<Media
  image={{ src: '/path/to/image.png', alt: 'Picture', disableCompress: true }}
/>

<Media
  youtube="https://youtu.be/0Qd3T6skprA"
  previewImg="/path/to/preview.png"
/>

<Media
  video={{
    src: ['/path/to/video.webm', '/path/to/video.mp4'],
    loop: { start: 0 }
  }}
/>

<Media
  iframe={{ src: 'https://www.google.com/maps/embed/', name: 'map' }}
/>

name: MetaInfo
description: Renders a row of meta-info items (dates, tags, etc.) as YFM-formatted text inside an h4 heading.
when_to_use: When displaying article or post metadata such as publication date or category tags
avoid: Avoid for primary headings; it uses h4 semantically for metadata
examples: <MetaInfo items={['one', 'two', 'three']} />

name: OutsideClick
description: A class component that detects clicks outside its wrapped content and fires an onOutsideClick callback.
when_to_use: When a dropdown or overlay should close when clicking outside its bounds
avoid: Avoid for accessibility-critical dismiss patterns; supplement with keyboard events

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler for the wrapper element.

examples: setDropdownOpen(false)}>

name: OverflowScroller
description: A horizontal scroll container with auto-detecting arrow navigation buttons that appear when content overflows the container width.
when_to_use: When a horizontal list of items may overflow and needs arrow-button scrolling
avoid: Avoid for vertical scrolling or when native scroll is acceptable

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the scroll container.

examples:
Item 1
Item 2
Item 3
Item 4
Item 5
Item 6

name: PageConstructor
description: The root component for rendering a full page from structured JSON content, managing block rendering, navigation, animation, branding, and custom block registration.
when_to_use: When rendering a full page from a PageContent data structure
When building a CMS-driven page with configurable blocks
avoid: Avoid for partial page sections; use individual block components instead

props.name: content
props.description: Page content data including blocks and background.

props.name: shouldRenderBlock
props.description: Predicate to conditionally skip rendering blocks.

examples:
<PageConstructor
  content={{
    blocks: [
      {
        type: 'header-block',
        title: 'Welcome',
        description: 'Page description text'
      }
    ]
  }}
/>

<PageConstructor
  content={{ blocks: pageBlocks }}
  navigation={navigationData}
  isBranded={true}
/>

name: ReactPlayer
description: A fully-featured video player component wrapping react-player with custom controls, mute/unmute, progress tracking, preview image, autoplay, and analytics.
when_to_use: When embedding an HTML5 or YouTube video with custom playback controls
When video needs autoplay-muted, progress tracking, or custom play button UI
avoid: Avoid for YouTube-embed-only use cases; VideoBlock is simpler
Avoid for audio-only content

props.name: autoRatio
props.description: Auto-calculate aspect ratio from video metadata.

props.name: customBarControlsClassName
props.description: CSS class for custom bar controls.

props.name: elapsedTime
props.description: Seek to this time in seconds.

props.name: loop
props.description: Loop playback, optionally within a time range.

examples:
<ReactPlayer
  src="/path/to/video.mp4"
  loop={true}
  showPreview={true}
/>

<ReactPlayer
  src={['/path/to/video.webm', '/path/to/video.mp4']}
  autoplay={true}
  muted={true}
  previewImgUrl="/path/to/preview.png"
/>

name: RouterLink
description: A thin wrapper that renders a router-aware Link from LocationContext if configured, otherwise renders children as a Fragment.
when_to_use: When a link should use the project's client-side router configured via LocationContext
avoid: Avoid when server-side or plain anchor navigation is sufficient; use a regular <a> instead
examples: About us

name: Table
description: Renders a data table from a 2D content array with optional legend, marker types (disk, tick), and configurable column justification.
when_to_use: When displaying structured tabular data with an optional legend and row/column markers
avoid: Avoid for complex interactive tables; this is a static presentational component
examples:
<Table
  content={[
    ['Feature', 'Free', 'Pro', 'Enterprise'],
    ['Users', '0', '0', '0'],
    ['Storage', '0', '1', '1'],
    ['Support', '0', '1', '1']
  ]}
  legend={['Free', 'Pro']}
  justify={['start', 'center', 'center', 'center']}
/>

<Table
  content={[
    ['Feature', 'Basic', 'Advanced'],
    ['Analytics', '0', '1'],
    ['API access', '1', '1']
  ]}
  marker="tick"
  hideLegend={true}
/>

name: Title
description: Renders a block title with an optional subtitle, using configurable grid column sizes and justification. Subtitle is rendered as YFM content.
when_to_use: When rendering the title and subtitle of a page block
avoid: Avoid for inline headings outside of block context; use plain heading tags instead

props.name: colJustifyContent
props.description: Justify content for the grid column.

props.name: title
props.description: Title text or full title configuration object.

examples:
<Title
  title="Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit"
  subtitle="**Ut enim ad minim veniam** [quis nostrud](https://example.com) exercitation ullamco laboris."
/>

<Title
  title={{ text: 'Lorem ipsum', url: 'https://example.com', urlTitle: 'Opens in a new window' }}
/>

name: ToggleArrow
description: Renders a directional arrow icon that animates between open/closed states, used for accordion toggles and scroll navigation.
when_to_use: When an accordion or collapsible element needs a visual arrow indicator
When a scrollable container needs left/right navigation arrows
avoid: Avoid for non-toggle arrow decorations; use Icon directly instead
examples: <ToggleArrow type="vertical" iconType="default" size={30} open={isOpen} />
<ToggleArrow type="horizontal" iconType="navigation" size={30} />

name: UnpublishedLabel
description: Displays an 'unpublished' indicator label or inline line marker, used to visually mark content that is not yet published.
when_to_use: When content blocks need a visual indicator that they are unpublished drafts
avoid: Avoid for status badges in application UI; use uikit Label or Badge instead
examples: <UnpublishedLabel type="label" />
<UnpublishedLabel type="line" />

name: VideoBlock
description: Renders a YouTube or custom iframe video with optional preview image and play button. Handles autoplay with AUTOPLAY_ATTRIBUTES and responsive height.
when_to_use: When embedding a YouTube video or custom iframe video with a preview thumbnail
avoid: Avoid for HTML5 file-based video; use ReactPlayer instead

props.name: autoplay
props.description: Enable autoplay when preview is clicked.

props.name: playButtonId
props.description: Id for the custom play button.

props.name: previewImg
props.description: Preview image URL shown before play.

props.name: stream
props.description: YouTube live stream channel ID or URL.

examples:
<VideoBlock
  record="https://youtu.be/0Qd3T6skprA"
  previewImg="/path/to/preview.png"
  attributes={{ color: 'white', rel: '0' }}
/>

name: YFMWrapper
description: Renders YFM (Yandex Flavored Markdown) HTML content via the HTML component, applying yfm CSS class with optional modifiers.
when_to_use: When rendering pre-compiled YFM HTML from a content pipeline
When page-constructor block text needs consistent YFM styling
avoid: Avoid for plain strings without YFM markup; use HTML or plain text instead

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: CSS class applied to the YFM content element.

props.name: modifiers
props.description: BEM modifiers applied to the yfm class.

name: YandexForm
description: Embeds a Yandex Forms iframe that auto-sizes, supports locale, theme, custom params, and fires analytics on form submission.
when_to_use: When embedding a Yandex Forms survey or contact form in a page block
avoid: Avoid for Hubspot forms; use HubspotForm instead
Avoid when Yandex Forms is not configured as the form provider

props.name: headerHeight
props.description: Header height offset for scroll after submit.

props.name: params
props.description: Extra query parameters for the form URL.

examples:
<YandexForm
  id="61a4e639d4d24e0dbba36f5c"
  customFormSection="cloud"
/>

name: useAnalytics
description: Returns an analytics event dispatcher function that sends events via AnalyticsContext. Supports a default event name/target and additional custom events.
when_to_use: When a component needs to fire analytics events through the page-constructor analytics pipeline
avoid: Avoid outside of page-constructor component tree where AnalyticsContext is not provided
signature: useAnalytics(name?: string, target?: string): (e?: AnalyticsEventsProp | null, additionalContext?: Record<string, string>) => void
examples:
const handleAnalytics = useAnalytics('button-click', 'cta');

<button onClick={() => handleAnalytics()}>Click me</button>

name: useDeviceValue
description: Returns the value appropriate for the current device (mobile/tablet/desktop) from a DeviceSupporting<T> value using the window width from WindowWidthContext.
when_to_use: When a prop or value needs to differ per device breakpoint
avoid: Avoid when a CSS-only media query solution is sufficient

name: useFocus
description: Tracks hover/focus state on an HTML element, adapting to mobile (pointerdown) vs desktop (mouseenter/mouseleave) events.
when_to_use: When a component needs to know if the user is hovering or focusing an element, with mobile-aware detection
avoid: Avoid for keyboard focus tracking; this hook is pointer/hover focused
signature: useFocus(element?: HTMLElement): { hasFocus: boolean; setFocus: () => void; unsetFocus: () => void }

name: useHeightCalculator
description: Measures and tracks the offsetHeight of a referenced element, updating on window resize with configurable debounce delay.
when_to_use: When a component needs to know the pixel height of a child element for animated height transitions
avoid: Avoid when ResizeObserver is available and preferred over window resize events
signature: useHeightCalculator(containerRef: React.RefObject<HTMLElement>, options?: { recalculateOnResizeDelay: number }): number | undefined

name: useMount
description: Runs an effect only on component mount (empty dependency array), with optional cleanup via the returned unsubscribe function.
when_to_use: When side effects should run exactly once on component mount
avoid: Avoid when the effect depends on props or state; use useEffect with dependencies instead

name: useWindowBreakpoint
description: Returns the current window width breakpoint value from the BREAKPOINTS constant, updating on window resize with debounce.
when_to_use: When a component needs to react to window breakpoint changes
avoid: Avoid when CSS media queries alone are sufficient

name: BackgroundCard
description: A card sub-block with a background image or color overlay, supporting title, text, links, buttons, and optional footer controls. Used inside CardLayoutBlock.
when_to_use: When a card needs a background image or color behind its content
Inside CardLayoutBlock for grid of image-backed cards
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: urlTitle
props.description: Accessible title for the card link.

props.name: additionalInfo
props.description: Small notice text below main content.

examples:
<BackgroundCard
  title="Feature"
  text="Description text."
  background={{ src: '/path/to/bg.png' }}
  url="/feature"
/>

name: BannerBlock
description: A full-width marketing banner block that wraps a BannerCard in an AnimateBlock. Displays a title, subtitle, CTA button, and optional background image.
when_to_use: When a page needs a prominent banner section with a headline and CTA
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: button
props.type: { url: string; text: string; target?: string; theme?: string }

props.name: mediaView
props.description: How much of the banner the image covers.

examples:
<BannerBlock
  title="Start for free"
  subtitle="No credit card required."
  button={{ url: '/signup', text: 'Get started' }}
  image="/path/to/banner-bg.png"
/>

name: BannerCard
description: The inner card component of BannerBlock. Renders title, subtitle, CTA button, and background image with theme support. Used directly when composing custom banners.
when_to_use: When embedding a banner card inside a custom layout
Used internally by BannerBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: button
props.type: { url?: string; text?: string; target?: string; theme?: string }

examples:
<BannerCard
  title="Try it now"
  button={{ url: '/demo', text: 'Request demo' }}
  image="/path/to/bg.png"
  theme="dark"
/>

name: BasicCard
description: A simple card sub-block with optional icon, title, text, links, buttons, and list. Wraps content in CardBase. Used inside CardLayoutBlock.
when_to_use: For feature grids and icon-based cards on landing pages
Inside CardLayoutBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<BasicCard
  title="Feature"
  text="Describe the feature here."
  icon={{ src: '/path/to/icon.svg' }}
  buttons={[{ text: 'Learn more', url: '/feature' }]}
/>

name: CardLayoutBlock
description: A grid layout block that arranges child cards (BasicCard, BackgroundCard, etc.) in configurable columns with optional title, description, and background image.
when_to_use: When displaying a grid of cards on a landing page
As the container for BasicCard, BackgroundCard, ImageCard, MediaCard
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: colSizes
props.description: Column sizes per breakpoint (default all:12, sm:6, md:4).

props.name: children
props.description: Card components to display in the grid.

examples:
<CardLayoutBlock title={{ text: 'Features' }} colSizes={{ all: 12, sm: 6, md: 4 }}>
  <BasicCard title="Feature 1" text="Description" />
  <BasicCard title="Feature 2" text="Description" />
  <BasicCard title="Feature 3" text="Description" />
</CardLayoutBlock>

name: CompaniesBlock
description: Displays a grid of company logos with an optional title and description. Supports themed images for light/dark mode.
when_to_use: When showcasing partner or client logos on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<CompaniesBlock
  title={{ text: 'Trusted by' }}
  images={{ light: { src: '/logos-light.png' }, dark: { src: '/logos-dark.png' } }}
/>

name: Content
description: A sub-block that renders a structured content block: title, body text (YFM), label list, bullet list, links, buttons, and additional notice text. The core content primitive for most blocks.
when_to_use: As the text content area inside blocks like InfoBlock, ContentLayoutBlock, ExtendedFeaturesBlock, QuestionsBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

name: ContentLayoutBlock
description: A full-width content layout block for rich editorial sections. Renders a Content sub-block and optional file downloads, with configurable text width and background.
when_to_use: For editorial text sections with optional downloadable files on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: textContent
props.description: Text content props forwarded to Content sub-block.

props.name: textWidth
props.description: Column width of the text area (default 'm').

examples:
Detailed description.

', buttons: [{ text: 'Learn more', url: '/docs' }] }} textWidth="m" />

name: Divider
description: A simple horizontal spacer sub-block with configurable size and optional border line. Used between blocks to add visual breathing room.
when_to_use: When vertical spacing or a separator is needed between page blocks
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples: <Divider size="xl" />
<Divider size="m" border />

name: ExtendedFeaturesBlock
description: A feature grid block that renders items with icons, titles, text, links, and buttons arranged in configurable columns. Ideal for showcasing product features.
when_to_use: When displaying a grid of product features with icons on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of feature items with title, text, icon, links, buttons.

props.name: colSizes
props.description: Column widths per breakpoint (default all:12, sm:6, md:4).

examples:
<ExtendedFeaturesBlock
  title={{ text: 'Key features' }}
  items={[
    { title: 'Fast', text: 'Lorem ipsum.', icon: { src: '/icon.svg' } },
    { title: 'Secure', text: 'Lorem ipsum.', icon: { src: '/icon2.svg' } }
  ]}
/>

name: FilterBlock
description: A filterable card grid block. Renders a tab bar of tags and shows only cards matching the selected tag. Internally uses CardLayoutBlock.
when_to_use: When cards on a landing page need to be filtered by category or tag
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: allTag
props.description: Show an 'All' tag; pass string for custom label.

props.name: items
props.description: Items with a card and tags[] for filtering.

examples:
<FilterBlock
  tags={[{ id: 'web', label: 'Web' }, { id: 'mobile', label: 'Mobile' }]}
  allTag="All"
  items={[
    { tags: ['web'], card: { type: 'basic-card', title: 'Web card' } },
    { tags: ['mobile'], card: { type: 'basic-card', title: 'Mobile card' } }
  ]}
/>

name: FoldableListBlock
description: An accordion/FAQ-style block with a content pane on the left and expandable list items on the right. Each item has a title and collapsible text body.
when_to_use: For FAQ sections or collapsible feature lists on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: list
props.description: Bullet list in the left pane.

props.name: items
props.type: { title: string; text: string; link?: LinkProps; listStyle?: string }[]

examples:
It is a great product.

' }, { title: 'How does billing work?', text: '

Monthly subscriptions.

' } ]} />

name: FormBlock
description: A two-column block that pairs a content section with an embedded form (Yandex Forms or HubSpot). Supports background images and layout direction.
when_to_use: When a landing page section combines marketing copy with a lead-capture form
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: formData
props.description: Form configuration for Yandex or HubSpot.

props.name: customFormNode
props.description: Custom form JSX to render instead of formData.

examples:
We will respond within 24 hours.

' }} formData={{ type: 'hubspot', portalId: '123', formId: 'abc-def' }} />

name: HeaderBlock
description: The primary hero header block for landing pages. Renders a full-width header with title, description, buttons, background media, optional breadcrumbs, and a side image or video.
when_to_use: As the top-of-page hero section on a landing page
When a page needs a title with background image/video and CTA buttons
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: status
props.description: Status badge rendered inside the title.

examples:
<HeaderBlock
  title="Build faster with our platform"
  description="Everything you need to ship production-ready apps."
  buttons={[
    { text: 'Get started', url: '/signup', theme: 'action', size: 'xl' },
    { text: 'Learn more', url: '/docs', theme: 'outlined', size: 'xl' }
  ]}
  background={{ src: '/hero-bg.jpg' }}
/>

name: HeaderSliderBlock
description: A slider of HeaderBlock panels. Renders multiple hero headers in a Swiper carousel with optional arrows. Each slide is a full HeaderBlock.
when_to_use: When a landing page hero area should cycle through multiple headline messages
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of header block configurations for each slide.

examples:
<HeaderSliderBlock
  items={[
    { title: 'Slide 1', description: 'First message.', buttons: [{ text: 'CTA', url: '/' }] },
    { title: 'Slide 2', description: 'Second message.', buttons: [{ text: 'CTA', url: '/' }] }
  ]}
  arrows
/>

name: HubspotForm
description: Embeds a HubSpot form by portalId and formId. Handles theming, mobile context, analytics on submit, and lifecycle hooks (onLoad, onBeforeSubmit, onSubmit).
when_to_use: When embedding a HubSpot marketing form inside a page-constructor page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: formInstanceId
props.description: Unique instance ID for multi-form pages.

props.name: formClassName
props.description: CSS class applied to the form element.

examples:
<HubspotForm
  portalId="12345678"
  formId="aaaabbbb-cccc-dddd-eeee-ffffffffffff"
  onSubmit={() => console.log('submitted')}
/>

name: IconsBlock
description: Renders a row of icon+label items with optional links. Each item shows a themed image and text; if a URL is provided the item becomes an anchor link.
when_to_use: For icon-link grids such as technology logos or social links on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of { src, text, url?, analyticsEvents? }.

examples:
<IconsBlock
  title={{ text: 'Integrations' }}
  items={[
    { src: '/icons/react.svg', text: 'React', url: 'https://react.dev' },
    { src: '/icons/ts.svg', text: 'TypeScript', url: 'https://typescriptlang.org' }
  ]}
/>

name: ImageCard
description: A card sub-block with a prominent image at the top, followed by optional title, text, links, and buttons. Supports clickable card via url prop.
when_to_use: For blog post previews, product image cards, or image+text cards inside CardLayoutBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

name: InfoBlock
description: A two-column info block with a left content area (title, text, buttons) and a right content area (links, list). Supports a background color.
when_to_use: For two-column informational sections on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: title
props.description: Left column heading (also used as sectionsTitle fallback).

props.name: leftContent
props.description: Full content override for the left column.

props.name: rightContent
props.description: Full content override for the right column.

examples:
We help teams ship faster.

', buttons: [{ text: 'Docs', url: '/docs' }] }} rightContent={{ links: [{ text: 'Pricing', url: '/pricing', arrow: true }] }} />

name: LayoutItem
description: A flexible sub-block composing media (image/video), meta info, icon, and content (title, text, links, buttons). Used inside CardLayoutBlock for rich card layouts.
when_to_use: For rich cards combining media and content inside CardLayoutBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: content
props.description: Text content (title, text, links, buttons, etc.).

props.name: metaInfo
props.description: Meta info row (date, author, tags, etc.).

examples:
Summary text.

', links: [{ text: 'Read more', url: '/article' }] }} metaInfo={[{ type: 'date', value: '2024-01-01' }]} />

name: MapBlock
description: Embeds a map iframe (e.g. Yandex Maps) inside a page block with a title. The map source is passed as a URL.
when_to_use: When a landing page needs to show a geographic map (office location, event venue)
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<MapBlock
  title="Our office"
  src="https://api-maps.yandex.ru/services/constructor/..."
/>

name: MediaBlock
description: A block for displaying a primary media asset (image or video) with an optional title, description, and border. Starts video playback on scroll.
when_to_use: When a landing page section should highlight a product screenshot, demo video, or hero image
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<MediaBlock
  title={{ text: 'See it in action' }}
  media={{ image: { src: '/demo.png', alt: 'Product demo' } }}
  border="shadow"
/>

name: MediaCard
description: A card sub-block that wraps a Media component inside a CardBase. Used to display image or video cards in a card grid.
when_to_use: When a grid item should display media (image/video) as a card inside CardLayoutBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<MediaCard
  image={{ src: '/screenshot.png', alt: 'Screenshot' }}
  border="shadow"
/>

name: Navigation
description: The site navigation component for page-constructor pages. Renders a responsive header nav with logo, left/right item groups, desktop dropdown menus, and a mobile sidebar.
when_to_use: As the top navigation bar on a page-constructor landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: data
props.description: Navigation data: leftItems, rightItems, withBorder, iconSize, etc.

props.name: mobilePortalContainer
props.description: Container ref for mobile sidebar portal.

examples:
<Navigation
  logo={{ src: '/logo.svg', href: '/', text: 'My Product' }}
  data={{
    leftItems: [
      { type: 'link', text: 'Docs', url: '/docs' },
      { type: 'link', text: 'Pricing', url: '/pricing' }
    ],
    rightItems: [
      { type: 'button', text: 'Sign up', url: '/signup', theme: 'action' }
    ],
    withBorderOnScroll: true
  }}
/>

name: PriceCard
description: A pricing card sub-block showing a plan title, price, period, feature list, buttons, and links. Supports themes and background color.
when_to_use: For pricing plan cards on a landing page inside CardLayoutBlock
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<PriceCard
  title="Pro"
  price="$49"
  pricePeriod="month"
  list={['Unlimited projects', '10GB storage', 'Priority support']}
  buttons={[{ text: 'Start free trial', url: '/signup/pro', theme: 'action' }]}
/>

name: PriceDetailed
description: A detailed pricing comparison component (deprecated). Renders price descriptions and feature details in combined or separate view layouts. Use PriceCard for new projects.
when_to_use: For legacy pricing comparison tables. Prefer PriceCard for new projects.
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI. Deprecated — prefer PriceCard.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of price items with description and details.

props.name: priceType
props.description: Type of price details (settings or list).

props.name: numberGroupItems
props.description: Items per group in combined view (default 1).

name: PromoFeaturesBlock
description: A masonry grid of feature cards with optional media (image/video). Each card has a title, text, and optional media. Uses BalancedMasonry for column layout.
when_to_use: For promotional feature showcases with mixed-height media cards on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.type: { title: string; text: string; media?: ThemedValue<MediaProps>; theme?: string }[]

examples:
<PromoFeaturesBlock
  title={{ text: 'Why choose us' }}
  items={[
    { title: 'Fast', text: 'Ships in milliseconds.', media: { image: { src: '/fast.png' } } },
    { title: 'Reliable', text: '99.9% uptime.' }
  ]}
/>

name: QuestionsBlock
description: An FAQ block with a content pane on the left and schema.org-marked-up accordion questions on the right. Supports multiple open items simultaneously.
when_to_use: For FAQ sections on landing pages with schema.org markup for SEO
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: list
props.description: Bullet list in the left pane.

props.name: items
props.type: { title: string; text: string; link?: LinkProps; listStyle?: string; onClick?: (index: number, isOpened: boolean) => void }[]

examples:
Sign up at example.com.

' }, { title: 'Is there a free plan?', text: '

Yes, up to 3 projects.

' } ]} />

name: Quote
description: A testimonial/quote card sub-block. Displays a quote text, logo, author avatar/name, and optional CTA button. Supports light/dark themes and border styles.
when_to_use: For customer testimonials, press quotes, or partner endorsements on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: yfmText
props.description: Quote text as YFM HTML (overrides text).

examples:
<Quote
  text="This product changed how we work."
  author={{ firstName: 'Jane', secondName: 'Doe', description: 'CEO at Acme', avatar: '/avatar.png' }}
  logo={{ src: '/acme-logo.svg' }}
  buttonText="Read case study"
  url="/case-studies/acme"
/>

name: ShareBlock
description: A social sharing block that renders icon buttons for Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, VK, and Telegram. Uses the current page URL to generate share links.
when_to_use: At the bottom of an article or blog post to let users share the page on social media
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.description: Social networks to show sharing buttons for.

examples:
<ShareBlock
  title="Share this article"
  items={['twitter', 'linkedin', 'facebook']}
/>

name: SliderBlock
description: A Swiper-powered carousel block. Renders children as slides with configurable dots, arrows, autoplay, breakpoints, and animation. Built on top of swiper/react.
when_to_use: When multiple cards or content panels should be navigable as a carousel on a landing page
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: children
props.description: Slide content — each child becomes one slide.

props.name: slidesToShow
props.description: Number of slides visible at once.

props.name: initialSlide
props.description: Index of the initial active slide (default 0).

examples:
<SliderBlock title={{ text: 'Case studies' }} arrows dots slidesToShow={3}>
  <BasicCard title="Case 1" text="Outcome." />
  <BasicCard title="Case 2" text="Outcome." />
  <BasicCard title="Case 3" text="Outcome." />
</SliderBlock>

name: TableBlock
description: A block that renders a Table component with a title in a two-column grid. Includes schema.org Table microdata for SEO.
when_to_use: For feature comparison or data tables on a landing page with SEO-friendly schema markup
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.
examples:
<TableBlock
  title="Feature comparison"
  table={{
    legend: ['Feature', 'Free', 'Pro'],
    rows: [
      ['Unlimited projects', false, true],
      ['Priority support', false, true]
    ]
  }}
/>

name: TabsBlock
description: A tabbed content block. Renders a ButtonTabs tab bar and a content panel per tab with media and text. Tabs can show media on the left or right of the content.
when_to_use: When a landing page section should show different content panels selected by a tab bar
avoid: For landing/marketing pages only. Not for application UI.

props.name: items
props.description: Tab items — each has tabName, text, media, links, buttons, etc.

props.name: direction
props.description: Layout: media left+content right, or reversed (default 'media-content').

props.name: tabsColSizes
props.description: Column widths for the tab bar.

examples:
Run npm install.

', media: { image: { src: '/step1.png' } } }, { tabName: 'Step 2', title: 'Configure', text: '

Set your config.

', media: { image: { src: '/step2.png' } } } ]} />
